english: — 2. to embroider C. — 3. to heal, rma (rma) wounds S.g. — tsem-drúb (tshem drub) needle-work Sch.

pf. drul (drul), gen. rul (rul) (q.v), to become putrid, to rot, to putrefy, drúl-bar (drul bar) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) id.; odrúl-bar ('drul bar) byéd-pa (byed pa) to cause to be decomposed Med.; rten-odrúl (rten 'drul) prob.: putrefied substances, bśań-gci-s (bshang gci s) obyiń-par-byed ('bying par byed) are removed with the faeces Med.

also lhá-odre (lha 'dre), W. *lán-ṭle*, goblin, gnome, imp, demon, evil spirit, devil, col. the most frq. word for such beings; quite in a general sense: klu-gñán-la (klu gnyan la) sógs-pai (sogs pa'i) lha-odre-rnams (lha 'dre rnams); byá-odre (bya 'dre), odre-rgód ('dre rgod) Lt. prob. two particular species of demons; zá-odre (za 'dre) is said to be a word for 'owl'; odres ('dres) okyér-ba ('khyer ba) to be carried off by goblins Ma.; odres-gnód ('dres gnod), odrei ('dre'i) gnód-pa (gnod pa) mischief done by evil spirits; odre ('dre) oǰúg-pa ('jug pa) the entering of evil spirits, the state of possession; odré-žugs-pa ('dre zhugs pa) (W. *-kán*) one possessed by a devil, a demoniac; skród-pa (skrod pa) to cast out, odúl-ba ('dul ba) to subdue (devils). Comp. odre-oǰígs-śiń ('dre 'jigs shing) = gu-gul-śiń (gu gul shing), 'devil's fear', a resinous wood, by the burning of which goblins are smoked out. odre-pań-kã ('dre pang ka) n. of the fruit of sgóń-tog (sgong tog) Wdn. — *ḍe-pu* (or *bu?*)-*tsúb* (de pu tsub) W. whirlwind, water-spout. — odré-po ('dre po) a male devil, odré-mo ('dre mo) a female d., odré-bu ('dre bu) a young d., an imp Cs. — odre-me-bud ('dre me bud) ignis fatuus, will-o'the wisp, Jack with the lantern Schr. — odre-dmág ('dre dmag) a goblin host. — odre-lág ('dre lag) the left hand, the left side of the body being supposed to belong to the evil spirits C. — odré-śig ('dre shig) 'devil's louse', bed-bug C. — odre-srín ('dre srin) goblins and Rakshasas, demons in general, frq.

I. pf. and imp. odres ('dres), prop. vb. n. to bsré-ba (bsre ba), 1. to be mixed with, de (de) yñis (gnyis) odrés-(-na) ('dres na) Lt. if the two are mixed with each other; pyogs-gčíg-tu (phyogs gcig tu) odres ('dres) mixed together, miscellaneous Lex.; odres-mtsáms ('dres mtshams) (otsams ('tshams) Tar.) the 'limit of mixing', rgyá-mtso (rgya mtsho) dań (dang) gáń-gã (gang ga) odrés-mtsáms ('dres mtshams) the influx of the Ganga into the sea Tar. 178, 9; tsig (tshig) gžan (gzhan) ma (ma) odrés-par ('dres par) without mingling other talk with (the conversation); k'a (kha) dań (dang) sñiń (snying) ma (ma) odres ('dres) a man with whom word and sentiment differ, a hypocrite; čos (chos) dań (dang) čos (chos) ma (ma) yin-pa (yin pa) odres ('dres) right and wrong were mixed together; in an absol. sense: spyód-pa (spyod pa) odrés-te ('dres te) mú-stegs-par (mu stegs par) gyúr-to (gyur to) his course of life degenerated, and he became a Brahmanist Pth.; dúd-gro (dud gro) odrés-pa ('dres pa) an animal of a mixed race, half-breed, mongrel; ma (ma) odrés-par ('dres par) without any confounding or mixing together, sharply discriminating Mil.; ma-odrés-pa (ma 'dres pa) prob. pure, unadulterated. — 2. to interfere, to meddle with, *de lé-ka dań ma de* (de le ka dang ma de) W. do not meddle with that; to have intercourse with, to engage in, B. and col.; ráń-sems (rang sems) blá-ma (bla ma) odrés-pas ('dres pas) bde (bde) through your, the Lama's, intercourse with my soul, in your society, I am happy Mil ; gtam (gtam) odré-ba ('dre ba) id. II. erron. for gré-ba (gre ba) Pth.
to slide, glide, slip, *déd-de* (ded de) *gyel* (gyel) W., *odéd-tag(?)* ('ded tag) *śór-ne* (shor ne) *gyel* (gyel) C. he slipped and fell.

pf. drań(s) (drang(s)), fut. drań (drang), imp. droń(s) (drong(s)), 1. to draw, drag, pull, a carriage Glr.; a person by his arm Dzl.; drág-tu (drag tu) violently Dzl.; to draw tight, a rope Dzl.; to draw from, to pull out, an arrow out of a wound Glr.; to press or squeeze out, matter, pus, Med.; to tear out, gsóń-poirgyú-ma (gsong po'i rgyu ma) the intestines of a living person; fig. k'a-čig (kha cig) tser-sńón-gyi (tsher sngon gyi) rigs-suań (rigs su'ang) odren ('dren) some reckon it (lit. draw it) to the species of Meconopsis Wdn.; to cause, to effect, bde-čén (bde chen) felicity Thgy., skyúg-pa (skyug pa) vomiting Tar. — 2. to conduct, water (W. *rán-če* (ran che)); to lead, to guide; with or without sna (sna), lam (lam) odrén-pa ('dren pa) to direct a person in his way; also sbst. guide, odren-méd ('dren med) without a guide, without a king Dzl.; esp. to lead to happiness, felicity, frq.; opp. to lóg-odren-pa (log 'dren pa) q.v.; yúl-du-dmag (yul du dmag) to lead an army into a country, to wage war against it, frq. — 3. to cite, to quote, luń (lung) a religious authority Cs. — 4. to invite, a guest; to call, to go to meet; to cause to appear, to conjure up, a ghost, a deity; resp. spyán-odren-pa (spyan 'dren pa), gdán-odren- (gdan 'dren)
